You consistently rejected shows prioritizing visual grandeur (Foundation, Rings of Power, House of Dragon) while embracing those where human stakes drive narrative (Dark, The Expanse, The Last Kingdom). Even your comedy preferences follow this—rejecting 'emotional cotton candy' for shows with 'gravity.' This isn't genre preference—it's a fundamental storytelling philosophy: 'human truth matters most' regardless of setting. That principle remained immutable across all 150 messages and 22 sessions.
